items.summary: The Xteink X4 and smaller X3 e-readers are discounted to $55.20 (regularly $69) and $63.20 (normally $79), respectively, as part of Amazon’s Prime Day promotions this week. items.title: ByteDance unveils Seedance 2.5, a 30-second native 4K AI video model that accepts 50 reference inputs
items.summary: ByteDance unveiled Seedance 2.5 on Tuesday at its Volcano Engine FORCE conference in Beijing, a video generation model that produces 30-second clips at native 4K resolution from a single prompt. items.title: Looking for a good deal on a new ThinkPad? I found one that's $830 off
items.summary: The Lenovo ThinkPad E16 (Gen 3) is on sale for $1,319 for Amazon Prime Day - nearly 40% off the regular price.

items.title: Prime Day has some solid deals on Switch 2, PS5, and Xbox games
items.summary: There are some some sizable discounts on new and recent hits from the last few years for the Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, and the Xbox Series X / S for Prime Day. items.title: This Garmin smartwatch is our top-selling Prime Day deal so far
items.summary: The Garmin Fenix 8 Pro is a luxury smartwatch with rugged features. ZDNET readers can't get enough of it on the first day of Prime Day.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Tag, an always-on AI teammate that lives in your Slack channels
items.summary: Anthropic is launching Claude Tag in research preview, an “always-on Claude” that lives inside Slack and acts as a persistent AI teammate. items.summary: The security implications of advanced AI models were immediately clear to Visa’s technology team when they began testing Anthropic’s Mythos model . items.title: Tesla claims driver ‘manually overrode self-driving’ in deadly Texas crash
items.summary: Tesla is pushing back on claims that its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system caused a fatal Texas crash, where a speeding Model 3 barreled into a home, killing a 76-year-old woman inside. items.title: A fake AI agent skill passed every security scanner and reportedly reached 26,000 agents
items.summary: Security firm AIR built a fake AI agent skill, pushed it through a popular skill marketplace and promoted it with an Instagram ad, and says it reached roughly 26,000 agents, including some on corporate accounts.
